title: Waste Not Compostable Caddy Liner, 100% Natural Material, 100% Biodegradable Bin Bags, 10L Capacity, Roll of 20
features: Perfect Fit for Food Waste Caddies: Waste Not compostable caddy liners hold up to 10 litres, fitting most standard kitchen food waste caddies securely, making them the ideal everyday liner for hassle-free food waste disposal at home.

100% Natural Material, No Plastic: Made entirely from natural materials with no plastic content, these caddy liners are a completely clean and eco-conscious alternative to conventional plastic bin liners for your kitchen food waste caddy.

Made from Vegetable Starch, Fully Biodegradable: Crafted from vegetable starch, these compostable liners break down naturally and completely, leaving no harmful residue behind and helping reduce your household plastic waste footprint with every bag you use.

Council Accepted & Home Compost Friendly: Accepted by many local councils for kerbside food waste collections and suitable for home composting, making these liners a versatile and responsible choice that fits seamlessly into your existing recycling routine.

Keeps Your Caddy Clean & Odour Free: Simply line your caddy with one of these liners and dispose of the whole bag when full, keeping your food waste caddy hygienic, fresh and free from mess, residue and unpleasant odours between collections.
description: From the manufacturer Waste Not Caddy Liners Waste Not compost caddy liners hold up to 10 litres and are made from 100% natural material. These compost liners are made from vegetable starch and are 100% biodegradable. Roll of 20 caddy liners. Range of Benefits Strong & Durable Less Mess Sustainable Roll of 20 liners With 10 litre capacity Easy to use Reduce the mess while also reducing your plastic waste 100% Biodegradable Composts entirely back to the earth Suitable for home composting Accepted by many councils for food waste and can be used for home composting too Help keep your caddy clean A simple solution to your messy compost caddy Made from vegetable starch Contributes towards reducing single-use plastics
